description: Improve platform developer tooling: README, CONTRIBUTING, unified check command, cross-platform publish script, version enforcement, type-check:e2e integration, e2e plugin prerequisite, tsconfig coverage check.

userStories.title: Create root README.md
userStories.description: As a new contributor, I want a standard README.md at the project root that helps me understand and get started with the project.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md exists at the project root
Contains: project name, one-line description, architecture overview (simplified from CLAUDE.md)
Contains: prerequisites (Bun version, Chrome), quick start (clone, install, build, load extension)
Contains: development commands table (build, type-check, lint, knip, test, test:e2e)
Contains: link to CLAUDE.md for detailed architecture documentation
Does not duplicate CLAUDE.md content — references it for details
userStories.notes:
Create a new file README.md at the project root. Keep it concise — CLAUDE.md is the detailed reference.

Structure:
1. # OpenTabs — one line: 'Chrome extension + MCP server that gives AI agents access to web applications through your browser.'
2. ## Quick Start — bun install, bun run build, load extension, opentabs start
3. ## Architecture — simplified 3-box diagram (Agent <-> MCP Server <-> Chrome Extension), brief explanation
4. ## Development — table of commands with descriptions
5. ## Directory Structure — compact tree showing platform/, plugins/, e2e/
6. ## Documentation — 'See CLAUDE.md for detailed architecture, patterns, and contribution guidelines.'

Do NOT enumerate individual plugins. Keep it plugin-agnostic per CLAUDE.md rules.

userStories.title: Create CONTRIBUTING.md
userStories.description: As a new contributor, I want clear contribution guidelines separate from the AI-focused CLAUDE.md.
acceptanceCriteria: CONTRIBUTING.md exists at the project root
Contains: how to set up the development environment
Contains: development workflow (bun run dev for server, manual extension reload)
Contains: verification checklist (all 5 commands that must pass)
Contains: code quality expectations (references CLAUDE.md code quality section)
Contains: how to add a new platform package
Contains: how to run E2E tests (including e2e-test plugin prerequisite)
userStories.notes:
Create CONTRIBUTING.md at the project root. This is the human-oriented companion to CLAUDE.md.

Structure:
1. ## Development Setup — prerequisites, git clone, bun install, bun run build
2. ## Development Workflow — `bun run dev` for full dev mode, or manual commands
3. ## Making Changes — server changes (hot reload), extension changes (manual reload), plugin changes (auto-notify)
4. ## Verification — all 5 commands, explain what each checks, note that pre-push hook runs 3 of them
5. ## Code Quality — brief list of key principles, link to CLAUDE.md for full rules
6. ## E2E Tests — must build e2e-test plugin first: `cd plugins/e2e-test && bun install && bun run build`
7. ## Adding a Platform Package — workspace setup, tsconfig wiring, reference from root
8. ## Publishing — reference scripts/publish.sh

Keep it practical and action-oriented.

userStories.title: Add unified verification command
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want a single command that runs all verification checks instead of running 5 separate commands.
acceptanceCriteria: Running 'bun run check' executes: build, type-check, lint, knip, test (in sequence)
If any command fails, the overall check fails and prints which step failed
The command is added to package.json scripts
The exit code reflects the first failure
userStories.notes:
Edit package.json at the project root. Add a new script:

```json
"check": "bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run knip && bun run test"
```

This chains all 5 commands with &&, so the first failure stops the chain. Simple and effective.

Optionally also add:
```json
"check:all": "bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run type-check:e2e && bun run lint && bun run knip && bun run test && bun run test:e2e"
```
For the comprehensive version including E2E.

userStories.title: Make publish script cross-platform
userStories.description: As a contributor on Linux, I want the publish script to work without macOS-specific sed syntax.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/publish.sh works on both macOS and Linux
The sed -i '' (macOS) vs sed -i (GNU) difference is handled
All version bumping and cross-reference updates work correctly on both platforms
userStories.notes:
Edit scripts/publish.sh. The issue is on lines 43, 49, 50, 53, 54, 80 — all use `sed -i ''` which is macOS-specific.

Two approaches:

1. **Detect platform and use correct syntax:**
```bash
if [[ "$OSTYPE" == "darwin"* ]]; then
  SED_INPLACE=(sed -i '')
else
  SED_INPLACE=(sed -i)
fi
"${SED_INPLACE[@]}" 's/pattern/replacement/' file
```

2. **Use a portable workaround:**
```bash
# Works on both macOS and Linux:
sed -i.bak 's/pattern/replacement/' file && rm file.bak
```

Approach 2 is simpler. sed -i.bak creates a backup with .bak extension on both platforms, then we remove the backup.

Apply to all sed -i calls in the script.

userStories.title: Remove redundant build from publish script
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want the publish script to not build twice unnecessarily.
acceptanceCriteria: The publish script runs bun run build only once (after version bumping, not before)
The post-bump build ensures all artifacts have the correct version
userStories.notes:
Edit scripts/publish.sh. Currently the script runs `bun run build` on line 38 (before version bump) and line 58 (after version bump). The first build is unnecessary — the version bump only changes package.json files, and the post-bump build will compile everything fresh.

Remove lines 37-38 (the first build step). Keep only the post-bump build on line 58.

userStories.title: Add automated versioning with git tags
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want the publish script to automatically create git commits and tags for releases.
acceptanceCriteria: After successful publish, the script creates a git commit with message 'release: v<version>'
The script creates a git tag 'v<version>'
The script does NOT push (leaves that to the developer)
The script prints next steps: 'git push && git push --tags'
If git working directory is dirty before publish, the script warns and asks for confirmation
userStories.notes:
Edit scripts/publish.sh. Add at the end (after the publish steps):

```bash
echo ""
echo "==> Creating release commit and tag..."
git add platform/shared/package.json platform/plugin-sdk/package.json platform/plugin-tools/package.json platform/cli/package.json
git commit -m "release: v$VERSION"
git tag "v$VERSION"

echo ""
echo "==> Release v$VERSION committed and tagged."
echo ""
echo "Next steps:"
echo "  git push && git push --tags"
echo "  Update plugin dependencies and rebuild"
```

Also add a pre-publish dirty check at the top:
```bash
if [ -n "$(git status --porcelain)" ]; then
  echo "Warning: git working directory has uncommitted changes."
  read -p "Continue anyway? [y/N] " confirm
  [[ "$confirm" == [yY] ]] || exit 1
fi
```

userStories.title: Enforce Bun version locally
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want a clear error when my local Bun version doesn't match the project's required version.
acceptanceCriteria: package.json has an 'engines' field specifying the minimum Bun version: >= 1.3.9
A clear error or warning appears when using an incompatible Bun version
CI Bun version (1.3.9) is consistent with the engines field
userStories.notes:
Edit the root package.json. Add an engines field:

```json
"engines": {
  "bun": ">= 1.3.9"
}
```

Bun respects the engines field in package.json and will print a warning if the running version doesn't match. This is a lightweight enforcement — it warns but doesn't block.

Note: Bun's engines support may vary. Verify that Bun actually reads and respects this field. If not, an alternative is to add a version check at the top of scripts/dev.ts or as a prepare script.

userStories.title: Include E2E type-check in main type-check command
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want bun run type-check to also check E2E test types so I don't forget to run type-check:e2e separately.
acceptanceCriteria: Running 'bun run type-check' checks both platform packages and E2E tests
The E2E tsconfig is included in the root tsconfig.json references (if not already)
The separate type-check:e2e command still exists for targeted checking
userStories.notes:
Check if e2e/tsconfig.json is already in the root tsconfig.json references. Looking at the root tsconfig.json (line 34): `{ "path": "e2e" }` — it IS already referenced.

So `tsc --build` (which is what `bun run type-check` runs) should already include e2e types. The issue might be that the e2e tsconfig uses noEmit or has different settings that prevent it from being included in --build.

Check e2e/tsconfig.json to understand why type-check:e2e is separate. If the e2e tsconfig is already in the root references, then `bun run type-check` should cover it. If there's a reason it was separated (e.g., different module system, node types vs bun types), document why.

If the e2e tsconfig is already covered by `tsc --build`, then the fix is simply to remove the separate `type-check:e2e` script and update CI to not call it separately. Or keep both and just update documentation.

userStories.title: Enforce E2E test plugin build as prerequisite
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want E2E tests to clearly fail with instructions if the e2e-test plugin isn't built.
acceptanceCriteria: Running 'bun run test:e2e' checks for plugins/e2e-test/dist/tools.json before running Playwright
If the file is missing, prints a clear error: 'E2E test plugin not built. Run: cd plugins/e2e-test && bun install && bun run build'
The check is fast and happens before Playwright launches

userStories.title: Add tsconfig coverage verification script
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want to verify that every .ts file in the repository is covered by a tsconfig that tsc --build reaches.
acceptanceCriteria: A script/command checks that all .ts/.tsx files under platform/ are covered by at least one tsconfig
The script can be run as 'bun run check:tsconfig-coverage' or similar
Files in node_modules/ and dist/ are excluded
If uncovered files are found, the script lists them and exits with code 1
The script is integrated into CI (added to the type-check job)
